# FM cockpit connect and add-agent entry: operable-cold onboarding surface

## Context

T5.4 of Epic #14560 (map v1.1 — adopted from the L2 co-drive handoff; my v1.0 missed it and *operable cold* is one of the SSOT's three product principles: "Add an agent = a name + a token"). Filed under the decomposition sprint (D#14561).

## The Problem

Today adding an agent or connecting a remote tenant is config-file surgery — the exact rockstar-only wall the adoption evidence names. The cockpit needs the entry surface where a stranger's first hour happens: add an agent (name + PAT + harness type), or connect a remote tenant (URL + PAT). This is also the design-partner cohort's literal entry point — the D3 path — which makes honesty here business-load-bearing, not just UX.

## The Architectural Reality

- Consumes Lane-D's add-agent flow (registry exists Brain-side per the cockpit plan; the UX does not) + #14574 (remote-tenant connect: tenant URL + PAT → remote MC/KB).
- **The credential boundary is non-negotiable** (the plan's two-hemisphere security rule): PATs go to the Node-side registry, encrypted at rest, never persisted browser-side; the form renders submission state honestly (#14611's round-trip discipline) and NEVER echoes the secret back.
- Validation UX per the self-config line (#14564's propose-confirm grammar): probe → propose → confirm — the entry surface renders what was detected before anything lands.
- ADR 0032 birth contract applies from the FIRST render: a newly added agent's card starts emergent (no mold), with emergence-parity affordances (§2.3.7).

## The Fix

The per-view triplet: (1) the entry surface (add-agent form + connect-tenant form; probe→propose→confirm flow; submission round-trip states); (2) unit specs — secret-never-echoed fixture, reject-with-reason renders, propose-step shows detected facts, new-card emergent render; (3) NL-verifiable add → the new resident appears in the grid with birth-contract conformance.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Add-agent: name + PAT + harness type → registry round-trip; the PAT is write-only in the UI (never re-rendered; fixture-asserted).
- [ ] Connect-tenant consumes #14574's contract; probe→propose→confirm renders detected facts before commit.
- [ ] Rejection paths render with reasons (bad token, unreachable tenant) — no silent failure.
- [ ] A newly added agent's card renders emergent per ADR 0032 §2.3.7 (no template inheritance — fixture).
- [ ] Unit specs (custom playwright config) + NL-verifiable end-to-end add.

## Out of Scope

Registry/keychain internals (Lane D) · #14574's wire implementation · tenant ingestion mechanics (KB-side).

## Related

Epic #14560 (parent) · blocked-by #14598 (card render for the new resident) · consumes #14574 + Lane-D flow · #14564 (grammar) · ADR 0032 §2.3.7 · D#14561.

Live latest-open sweep: fold-window fresh; no equivalent. A2A sweep: clean (the leaf was the L2 handoff's own suggestion). Structure-map gate: N/A — app-layer view.
